channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What to know about Linda McMahon, Trump’s pick for Education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon has been a constant presence in Donald Trump's tumultuous orbit, serving in his first administration and supporting his presidential campaigns. Now he's chosen her to serve as Education secretary. Here's a look at McMahon's background, from business to politics.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas offers Trump 1,400 borderland acres for deportation plans
item.description: Texas Land Commissioner Dawn Buckingham offered 1,402 acres in Starr County to the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration to announce $275 million Ukraine weapons package this week
item.description: The Biden administration is expected to announce a $275 million presidential drawdown authority weapons package for Ukraine this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-life groups cautious on RFK Jr. nomination after evolving abortion views
item.description: Pro-life groups are skeptical but optimistic about the nomination of pro-choice Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to lead the Department of Health and Human Services under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump Cabinet picks increase odds Edward Snowden could see life of freedom in the US
item.description: If Trump has his way, Tulsi Gabbard will be at the helm of U.S. intelligence and Matt Gaetz will be leading the DOJ, giving whistle-blower Edward Snowden his best chances yet at freedom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House yet to release visitor logs for month Biden dropped out of race
item.description: The White House has not released visitor logs for the month President Biden gave up his re-election bid, leaving questions on who was seeing the president before he dropped out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to nominate Mehmet Oz for CMS administrator
item.description: President-elect Trump said Tuesday he is nominating television personality Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) administrator. Oz unsuccessfully ran for Senate in Pennsylvania in 2022 with Trump's endorsement and has been a Trump ally ever since.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US will send Ukraine at least $275 million in new weapons in push to bolster Kyiv before Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon will send Ukraine at least $275 million in new weapons, U.S. officials said Tuesday, as the Biden administration rushes to do as much as it can to help Kyiv fight back against Russia before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Trump and Speaker Johnson could force the Senate to adjourn, allow recess appointments
item.description: Legal experts and members of the House GOP are at odds over whether Speaker Johnson and President-elect Trump could, and should, bypass the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump expected to pick Howard Lutnick for Commerce secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to nominate Howard Lutnick to serve as Commerce secretary, a source familiar told The Hill. Lutnick is the chair and CEO of Cantor Fitzgerald and is also currently serving as co-chair of the Trump transition.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House leaders agree to even split on committee control
item.description: Democratic and Republican legislative leaders will name members to the panels for the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: FEMA chief agrees to investigation after congressional hearing
item.description: Questions have focused on the agency’s preparedness and response to recent disasters, including Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Top Democrat on House Ethics panel says Gaetz report should be released
item.description: Rep. Susan Wild (D-Pa.), the top Democrat on the House Ethics Committee, said she thinks the panel’s report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) should be released.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Biden asks Congress for about $100 billion in disaster relief funding
item.description: The largest chunk of funding — about $40 billion — would be for FEMA's disaster relief fund, so that it has enough money to last through the coming year. But there are requests for 16 agencies.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Some in the U.S. farm industry are alarmed by Trump's embrace of RFK Jr. and tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump won landslide support in much of farm country, but his embrace of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his plan for a tariff fight with China alarms many farmers and agriculture experts.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Florida congressional leaders demand answers from FEMA political scandal
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - Republican and Democratic leaders on Capitol Hill are preparing for a hearing next week with FEMA administrator Deanne Criswell, regarding alleged political discrimination after hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What’s ahead for abortion access in Minnesota under a Trump administration
item.description: While detailed plans have yet to emerge, health and legal experts, practitioners and advocates in Minnesota are closely monitoring the new administration for hints about what federal changes to abortion could mean for the state.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to 'Make America Healthy Again.' He could face a lot of pushback
item.description: RFK Jr. wants to tackle chronic disease. Despite controversial views on vaccines, his focus on healthy food and taking on special interests may find broad support — and face political headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Allison Aubrey, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks former Georgia congressman for VA secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump has announced former Rep. Doug Collins (R-Ga.), a chaplain in the Air Force Reserve, as his pick for the next Secretary of Veterans Affairs.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court allows Indiana gender-affirming care ban to remain in effect
item.description: Indiana’s Republican Gov. Eric Holcomb signed the law, Senate Bill 480, last spring, one day after he called the measure “clear as mud.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5
